inputs.label: Area to Clean
inputs.businessContext: Total area to be cleaned in square meters. Based on industry standards for facility management (ISO 41001).

inputs.label: Cleaning Frequency
inputs.businessContext: How often cleaning is performed. Common frequencies in janitorial services.

inputs.label: Labor Rate per Hour
inputs.businessContext: Hourly wage for cleaning staff. Based on minimum wage and market rates (BLS data).

inputs.label: Labor Productivity
inputs.businessContext: Area cleaned per hour per worker. Typical range from industry benchmarks (ISSA).

inputs.label: Supplies Cost per sqm
inputs.businessContext: Cost of cleaning supplies (detergents, disinfectants) per square meter. Based on average consumption rates.

inputs.label: Equipment Cost per sqm
inputs.businessContext: Depreciation and maintenance cost of cleaning equipment per square meter. Includes vacuum cleaners, floor polishers.

inputs.label: Waste Disposal Cost per sqm
inputs.businessContext: Cost for waste removal and disposal per square meter. Based on local waste management rates.

inputs.label: Overhead Percentage
inputs.businessContext: Indirect costs (management, insurance, training) as percentage of direct costs. Typical range 15-30% in service industries.

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Confidence in input data accuracy. Affects adjustment factor for final cost.

thresholds.laborProductivity < 20: Low productivity may indicate inefficiency or need for training.
thresholds.suppliesCostPerSqm > 0.2: Supplies cost is high; consider bulk purchasing or alternative products.
thresholds.overheadPercentage > 40: Overhead is high; review indirect costs for potential savings.

suggestedActions: laborProductivity < 20 ? 'Consider training or better equipment to improve productivity.' : ''
suppliesCostPerSqm > 0.2 ? 'Negotiate bulk pricing or switch to cheaper supplies.' : ''
overheadPercentage > 40 ? 'Review overhead allocation and reduce indirect costs.' : ''
